news.headline: OTC Markets Group welcomes QNB Corp. to OTCQX Best Market from Pink market; operator trades 12,000 securities.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, OTC Markets Group Inc. revealed that QNB Corp., the parent company of QNB Bank, has been authorized to trade on the OTCQX® Best Market under the symbol OTCQX: QNBC. OTC Markets Group Inc. is known for overseeing regulated markets where around 12,000 U.S. and international securities are traded. This move signifies QNB Corp.'s upgrade from the Pink® market to the OTCQX platform, allowing for enhanced visibility and accessibility for investors.

news.headline: Zeekr Group acquires 30% of LYNK & CO for RMB5.4 billion and 20% for RMB3.6 billion. Its pro forma financial info includes data from 2023-2024.
news.summary: Zeekr Group, a leading premium new energy vehicle group, announced strategic integration transactions with Geely group, including LYNK & CO Acquisition and Capital Injection. Zhejiang Zeekr acquired 30% and 20% equity interest in LYNK & CO from Volvo Car (China) Investment Co., Ltd and Geely Holding, at purchase prices of RMB5.4 billion (US$740 million) and RMB3.6 billion (US$493 million) respectively. Zhejiang Zeekr also increased LYNK & CO’s registered capital by RMB367 million (US$50 million). After the transactions, LYNK & CO is owned 51% by Zeekr and 49% by Ningbo Geely. The transactions were completed on February 14, 2025. The pro forma financial information includes balance sheet data for 2023 and 2024, and income/cash flow data for 2024. However, it doesn’t include potential cost savings or revenue enhancements from the acquisition. Zeekr Group, from China, aims to build a full user ecosystem in the electric vehicle space with values of equality, diversity, and sustainability. Visit https://ir.zeekrgroup.com for more information.

news.headline: Solvang selects Inmarsat's NexusWave for crew and business connectivity across its gas carrier fleet.
news.summary: Inmarsat's comprehensive bonded connectivity service, NexusWave, is aiding Solvang's advanced gas carrier fleet by catering to their changing crew and business needs. The service ensures a fully managed connection, supporting operations across the fleet with efficiency.

news.headline: BD reaches milestone in clinical trial for bioabsorbable GalaFLEX LITE™ scaffold in breast implant revision surgery.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in Franklin Lakes, New Jersey, BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) disclosed that the initial patient has been treated in an Investigational Device Exemption (IDE) clinical trial. This trial marks a significant step forward for BD (NYSE: BDX), a prominent medical technology firm, as they strive to enhance their medical innovations.

news.headline: IREN restates financial statements for FYs 2022-2024, citing SEC review, now classifies Bitcoin sales as investing activities.
news.summary: IREN Limited (NASDAQ: IREN) revealed today in Sydney that its consolidated financial statements for the fiscal years ending June 30, 2024, 2023, and 2022, along with the unaudited interim condensed consolidated financial statements for the three months ending September 30, 2024, and the three and six months ending December 31, 2024, are to be amended and restated. This decision from the Company's Board of Directors comes after a review by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding its Form 20-F for the fiscal year concluded on June 30, 2024. The restatement involves categorizing proceeds from sales of Bitcoin mined as cash flows from investing activities, complying with IAS 7.16(b), "Statement of Cash Flows."

news.headline: Lindsay Corp (NYSE: LNN) to release Q2 fiscal 2025 results on April 3, 2025. Management to host earnings conference call.
news.summary: Lindsay Corporation, a global leader in irrigation and infrastructure equipment and technology, is set to report its financial performance for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, which concluded on February 28, 2025. The results are scheduled to be disclosed on Thursday, April 3, 2025, before the market opens. President and CEO Randy Wood and CFO Brian Ketcham will conduct a conference following the announcement. Lindsay Corporation is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol LNN.

news.headline: GEO Group gets 1,800-bed facility contract with ICE in Michigan, plan to finalize long-term deal in coming months.
news.summary: In Boca Raton, Florida, The GEO Group, Inc. revealed that it has signed a contract with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to promptly open a federal immigration processing center at their 1,800-bed North Lake Facility in Baldwin, Michigan. The Facility, owned by GEO, will be utilized for this purpose. A more permanent agreement between GEO and ICE is set to be completed in the coming months. GEO will be offering support services for ICE at the Facility.

news.headline: Caleres reports Q4 & full year 2024 results, expects growth in 2025. Gained market share in women's footwear.
news.summary: St. Louis-based Caleres, a prominent collection of consumer-focused shoe brands listed on the NYSE as CAL, has released its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and entirety of 2024 and has given insights into the first quarter and all of 2025. The company has communicated that its fourth quarter profits were in line with the upper range of their recent forecasts. They experienced an expansion in market presence for women's trendy footwear, saw strong performance from their Lead Brands, and managed to increase their sneaker market share. However, Famous Footwear's operations showed a slowdown during the quarter.

news.headline: Lands' End shows financial growth in Q4 & FY 2024. Q4 net revenue decreased to $441.7M from $514.9M in 2023. Gross margin up to 45.6% from 38%. Net income $18.5M in Q4, compared to Net loss $8.6M in 2023. For FY 2024, net revenue was $1.36B down from $1.47B in 2023. Net income of $6.2M compared to Net loss $130.7M in 2023. Cash and equivalents $18.8M compared to $27.3M in 2024 for FY 2024. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $92.6M from $84.3M in 2023. In the first quarter of fiscal 2025 Lands' End expects Net revenue between $260.0M and $290.0M, Gross Merchandise Value to deliver flat-to-low single digits growth, with a Net loss between $9.0M and $6.0M. Adjusted EBITDA expected in the range of $9.0M to $12.0M.
news.summary: Lands' End reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of fiscal 2024. In the fourth quarter, Net revenue was $441.7 million, with Gross profit at $201.3 million, representing a 3.0% increase from fiscal 2023. Gross margin also significantly improved by 760 basis points to 45.6%. Net income for the quarter was $18.5 million, a positive turnaround from a loss in the previous year. For the full fiscal year, Net revenue was $1.36 billion, with Gross profit at $653.3 million, marking a 4.4% increase from fiscal 2023. Gross margin improved by approximately 550 basis points to 47.9%. Full-year Net income was $6.2 million, a significant improvement from a loss of $130.7 million in fiscal 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter was $43.7 million and $92.6 million for the full fiscal year. Lands' End also saw a reduction in inventory for the eighth consecutive quarter, achieving a 12% year-over-year decrease. The company's cash and cash equivalents totaled $18.8 million as of January 31, 2025. Following these positive results, Lands' End provided an outlook for fiscal 2025, expecting growth in Net revenue, Gross Merchandise Value, and Adjusted EBITDA. The company emphasized its focus on generating improved cash flows and expanding its digital business and operations to drive further growth.

news.headline: Designer Brands Inc. reports Q4 and FY 2024 financial results, highlighting EPS at high end of guidance range. Q4 net sales -5.4% to $713.6M, FY net sales -2.1% to $3B. 2025 guidance: net sales growth, diluted EPS $0.30 - $0.50. Stores: 669 total. EPS 2024: $0.27. Cash: $44.8M. Shares repurchased: 10.3M. Dividend: $0.05/share. Webcast info provided. Company focusing on customer-centric approach for future growth.
news.summary: Designer Brands Inc. (NYSE: DBI) reported its financial results for the year ended February 1, 2025. The company's net sales for the full year of 2024 decreased by 2.1% to $3.0 billion, with a total comparable sales decrease of 1.7%. Gross profit also decreased to $1.29 billion, with reported net loss attributable to Designer Brands Inc. of $10.5 million. Adjusted net income was $15.0 million, with adjusted diluted earnings per share ("EPS") of $0.27.Cash and cash equivalents were $44.8 million at the end of 2024, and the company had debt totaling $491.0 million. In 2024, Designer Brands repurchased 10.3 million Class A common shares at a cost of $68.6 million.For the full year 2025, Designer Brands provided guidance, including a low-single-digit net sales growth and a diluted EPS between $0.30 and $0.50. The company is confident in its ongoing business transformation efforts. Additionally, the store count as of February 1, 2025, was 669 stores with a total square footage of 11,024. Designer Brands is a leading designer, producer, and retailer of footwear and accessories and has a diversified portfolio of brands. Investors and analysts can access the detailed financial information in the full article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: CMC reports Q2 fiscal 2025 results with $25.5M net earnings, $1.8B net sales; North America steel demand drives 3.3% increase in finished steel shipments compared to prior year Q2. Europe segment achieves adjusted EBITDA breakeven. Emerging Businesses Group sees profitability growth. Adjusted EBITDA for North America Steel Group decreases to $128.8M. Available liquidity nearly $1.6B. Quarter dividend of $0.18 per share declared, marked 242nd consecutive payment. IRVING, Texas — Commercial Metals Company announced financial results for its Q2 ended February 28, 2025, net earnings $25.5M, net sales $1.8B. Adjusted earnings $29.3M, adjusted EBITDA $131M. CMC strategy and investment contributing positively to fiscal 2025 performance. Total character count: 484
news.summary: Commercial Metals Company (CMC) reported second quarter net earnings of $25.5 million, or $0.22 per diluted share, on net sales of $1.8 billion, compared to prior year period net earnings of $85.8 million, or $0.73 per diluted share. Adjusted earnings were $29.3 million, or $0.26 per diluted share. The consolidated core EBITDA was $131.0 million with a core EBITDA margin of 7.5%. North American construction demand led to a 3.3% increase in finished steel shipments. New project awards reached the second-highest level since late fiscal 2022. Europe Steel Group achieved adjusted EBITDA breakeven. The Emerging Businesses Group saw increased profitability. The company repurchased $48.0 million in stock during the quarter. A quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share was declared. CMC expects improved financial results in the third quarter of fiscal 2025. Adjusted EBITDA for the Europe Steel Group is anticipated to remain near breakeven. The Emerging Businesses Group is expected to improve above the prior year period. The company's balance sheet and liquidity position remained strong, with cash equivalents totaling $758.4 million and available liquidity of nearly $1.6 billion. Adjusted EBITDA for the North America Steel Group decreased to $128.8 million in the second quarter. Adjusted EBITDA for the Europe Steel Group increased to $0.8 million. The Emerging Businesses Group achieved a 31.2% increase in adjusted EBITDA on a year-over-year basis. A quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share was declared, marking the 242nd consecutive quarterly payment by the company. Overall, CMC remains confident in the long-term fundamentals of the U.S. construction market.

news.headline: Switched Source invests $5 million in new manufacturing facility in Chicago, increasing production capacity by 150% to deliver at least 50 Phase-EQ units annually for grid modernization.
news.summary: A company specializing in power flow control technology, Switched Source, has opened a new 20,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in the Chicagoland area to address the rising grid limitations and energy needs faced by utilities. This expansion is set to enhance their current production capacity by 150%, enabling the company to manufacture a minimum of 50 units of its Phase-EQ technology annually. This advanced technology by Switched Source offers a cost-efficient solution to power flow challenges.

news.headline: Endace wins 5 accolades in 2025 Globee Awards for Cybersecurity, including Gold in Influencer Marketing and Cloud Computing.
news.summary: Endace, a packet capture specialist based in Austin, Texas and Auckland, New Zealand, was recently acknowledged in the 21st Annual 2025 Globee Awards for Cybersecurity. They achieved the GOLD award in the category of Influencer Marketing in Cybersecurity for their project titled "Packet Forensics Files: Sharing Insights and Trends from Cybersecurity Influencers." The Endace team members who led this initiative were Michael Morris, Senior Director of Technology Alliances; Gabby Steyn, Marketing Manager; and Mark Evans, VP Marketing. Additionally, they secured another GOLD award in the Cloud Computing Innovation category. This recognition showcases their expertise and commitment in the cybersecurity domain.

news.headline: Accenture's Q2 fiscal 2025 results announced, ending Feb 28, 2025. Key figures not provided.
news.summary: Accenture has released their financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, which concluded on February 28th, 2025.

news.headline: Lands’ End sees significant gross margin increase of 760 basis points in Q4 2024 compared to 2023; Eight consecutive quarters of reduced inventory.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter, there was a notable increase of around 760 basis points in gross margin compared to the previous fiscal year. All profitability measures for the quarter saw improvements in comparison to the same period in fiscal 2023. Additionally, inventory levels have been decreased for the eighth consecutive quarter.

news.headline: Titan Machinery Inc. reported $304M inventory reduction in Q4, totaling $419M since second quarter peak.
news.summary: In the fiscal fourth quarter, there was a significant achievement of reducing inventory by around $304 million. This brings the total inventory reduction from its peak in the fiscal second quarter to approximately $419 million.

news.headline: Stocks fall after Powell's speech, Dow Jones dips. Tesla, Nvidia, Boeing see gains in the market.
news.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell delivered a speech that appeased investors, but the response from the stock market was mixed. Following the address, Tesla, Nvidia, and Boeing saw an increase in their stock prices. Despite this positive trend for those companies, Dow Jones Futures experienced a decline. Investors are advised to keep an eye out for a specific indicator following Powell's rally to gauge the market's direction.

news.headline: Bitnomial is set to introduce the inaugural US XRP futures, as they withdraw their lawsuit against the SEC, noting regulatory enhancements.
news.summary: The cryptocurrency exchange has chosen to drop its lawsuit against the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on its own accord. The exchange highlighted the advancements in regulatory guidance as the reason behind its decision.

news.headline: Crypto market cap rises 3.2% to $2.8 trillion on March 20. Bitcoin up 3%, Ether up 4%. US equities gain, Dollar drops 6.04%. Fed leaves rates unchanged. Trump to address crypto policies. Bitcoin ETF inflows hit record $3.4 billion. Market eyes bullish breakout at $2.8-3 trillion. RSI shows bullish momentum. No investment advice provided.
news.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw a 3.2% increase in total market capitalization to $2.8 trillion on March 20, led by Bitcoin and Ether with 3% and 4% gains respectively. The rise was attributed to a risk-on sentiment following the Fed's decision to keep interest rates steady, with gains in US equities and crypto-related stocks like Coinbase and MicroStrategy. The US Dollar Index has dropped over 6% since Jan. 13. Speculation of a potential update to US cryptocurrency policy and President Trump's upcoming address at a crypto conference contributed to market optimism. Institutional interest in a crypto-friendly regulatory environment was highlighted, with Bitcoin rebounding above $85,000. BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes suggested $77,000 as Bitcoin's likely bottom. Technical analysis indicates a potential bullish breakout with the total market cap seeking to breach resistance levels at $2.8 trillion and $3 trillion. The article emphasized that readers should conduct their own research before making any investment decisions.

news.headline: US stock futures down post-Fed decision; 4 major indices showing decline in premarket trading. Nasdaq 100 -0.83%, S&P 500 -0.66%, Dow Jones -0.53%, Russell 2000 -0.75%. Indices have dropped from their 52-week highs, Nasdaq in correction territory. Fed projects 2 rate cuts by 2025, maintaining initial forecast. Treasury yields: 10-year at 4.22%, 2-year at 3.97%. Chance Fed will keep interest rates same in May: 82.6%. Notable stock rebounds post-Fed announcement include Nvidia (+1.8%) and Boeing (+7%). Major sectors affected by rate cut projections. Economists predict stagflation concerns due to lower growth and higher inflation. SPY up 0.29% to $568.76, QQQ up 0.40% to $482.80 in premarket trading. Expert advises that pullback in equities presents a buying opportunity.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures saw a decline following the FOMC's decision, with all four benchmark indices showing lower premarket trading. Futures for Nasdaq 100 dropped by 0.83%, S&P 500 by 0.66%, Dow Jones by 0.53%, and Russell 2000 by 0.75%. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.22%, with a 3.97% two-year yield. The FedWatch tool predicts an 82.6% chance of interest rates staying unchanged in May. The S&P 500 ETF Trust and QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket trading, with SPY at $568.76 (+0.29%) and QQQ at $482.80 (+0.40%). Notable stock movements included a 1.8% rebound by Nvidia Corp. and Boeing Co. rising by nearly 7%. Nasdaq 100 is in correction territory, down by 11.19% from its peak, while Dow Jones and S&P 500 have fallen by 6.70% and 7.68%, respectively, from their highs. The Nasdaq Composite showed 1.41% growth, the S&P 500 rose by 1.08%, the Dow Jones increased by 0.92%, and Russell 2000 went up by 1.57%. Market insight mentions concerns over stagflation due to lower growth and higher inflation signals from the Fed. Market volatility is driven by tariff uncertainties.

news.headline: South Korean prosecutors raid Bithumb over $2M embezzlement by ex-CEO for apartment purchase; firm facing probe amid IPO plans.
news.summary: South Korean prosecutors recently raided Bithumb, a crypto exchange, suspecting its former CEO Kim Dae-sik of embezzling funds to buy an apartment. Kim allegedly received a 3 billion Korean won apartment lease deposit from Bithumb. The Financial Supervisory Service had previously investigated the matter. Bithumb confirmed some allegations and stated that Kim repaid the funds. The exchange is facing a probe while pushing for an IPO, aiming to eliminate judicial risks. Bithumb's long-awaited IPO plans date back to 2020, with preparations ongoing since. The company faced obstacles but chose underwriters in 2023. In 2024, Bithumb Korea set up a non-exchange business to accelerate its stock market debut but saw a 57% loss in 2023 revenue.

news.headline: Baylin Technologies Inc. reports strong fiscal 2024 results: Revenue up 14.4% to $83.6M, gross profit up 21.1%, Adjusted EBITDA up $5.6M.
news.summary: Baylin Technologies Inc. reported strong financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. Revenue increased by $10.5 million, reaching $83.6 million, a 14.4% jump from fiscal 2023. Gross profit also rose by $6.0 million to $34.4 million, representing a 21.1% increase. Adjusted EBITDA saw a significant surge of $5.6 million, hitting $5.4 million. The gross margin improved to 41.1%, an increase of 5.7%. The net loss for the year was $8.5 million. Net debt stood at $14.3 million, with a backlog of $30.2 million at year-end. Revenue in the fourth quarter was $20.8 million, up 28.9% from the previous year. Gross profit increased by 38.8% to $7.9 million, with a gross margin of 37.9%. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $1.8 million, a stark improvement from the negative $2.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Net loss for the quarter was $4.9 million. Discontinued operations showed a net income of $3.7 million, a significant turnaround from previous losses. The company will hold an investor conference call on March 20, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. ET to discuss these results.

news.headline: Due Process Rights Under Attack in Recent Law Proposal, Experts Concerned.
Hyperscale Data declares $0.2708333 monthly dividend on Series D stock yielding 13%.
news.summary: Hyperscale Data has announced a monthly cash dividend of $0.2708333 per share for its 13.00% Series D Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock.

news.headline: SoundThinking renews contract with NYPD for TechnoLogic software, subsidiary serving enterprise resource and case management.
news.summary: SoundThinking, Inc. has renewed its partnership with the New York City Police Department for TechnoLogic, a subsidiary that offers enterprise resource and investigative case management software and services. The partnership is aimed at enhancing the effectiveness of law enforcement operations through innovative technological solutions.

news.headline: iOnctura begins Phase II trial for metastatic uveal melanoma with roginolisib. EMA grants Orphan Drug Designation offering 10 years market exclusivity in EU.
news.summary: The Phase II OCULE-01 study has initiated dosing for the first patient with roginolisib, an allosteric modulator of PI3Kδ. Notably, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) has awarded Orphan Drug Designation for roginolisib in the treatment of uveal melanoma. This designation offers significant advantages, including 10 years of market exclusivity specifically in the European Union, highlighting the potential impact of this drug in addressing the needs of patients with this condition.

news.headline: PDD Holdings reports strong financial results in Q4 2024: Revenues increased to RMB110.6 billion ($115.2 billion), net income grew by 18% to RMB27.4 billion ($3.8 billion). Company prioritizes platform ecosystem investments for long-term value creation. Total revenues for FY 2024 stood at RMB393.8 billion ($53.9 billion), with net income rising by 87% to RMB112.4 billion ($15.4 billion).
news.summary: PDD Holdings Inc. reported strong financial results for Q4 2024 and fiscal year 2024. In Q4 2024, total revenues were RMB110,610.1 million (US$115,153.5 million), up 24% from the same period in 2023. Operating profit was RMB25,592.2 million (US$3,506.1 million), a 14% increase from Q4 2023. Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders was RMB27,446.6 million (US$3,760.2 million), an 18% increase from the same period last year. For FY 2024, total revenues were RMB393,836.1 million (US$53,955.3 million), a 59% increase from 2023. Operating profit was RMB108,422.9 million (US$14,853.9 million), an 85% increase from 2023. Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders was RMB112,434.5 million (US$15,403.5 million), an 87% increase from 2023. Cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments also saw significant increases from 2023. The company emphasized its commitment to sustainable growth and investment in its platform ecosystem as key drivers of its success.

news.headline: HUYA Inc. sets July 1, 2025 as ex-dividend date for cash dividend announcement on NYSE. (Numbers: March 20, 2025)
news.summary: In Guangzhou, China on March 20, 2025, HUYA Inc. (NYSE: HUYA), a prominent game live streaming platform in China, disclosed that the New York Stock Exchange has established July 1, 2025 as the ex-dividend date for the Company’s cash dividend disbursement.

news.headline: Quarterhill Inc. to exhibit at 2025 IBTTA Tech Summit in Dallas as platinum sponsor. Event from March 22-25. Discussing tolling tech.
news.summary: Quarterhill Inc. has secured the position of platinum sponsor and exhibitor at the upcoming IBTTA Technology Summit scheduled to be held in Dallas, Texas from March 22 to 25, 2025. This summit serves as a crucial forum for discussions surrounding the evolving landscape of tolling technology. Quarterhill is poised to present its cutting-edge innovations and solutions during the event.

news.headline: Paradigm Health appoints Dr. Karen Knudsen to Board of Directors and unveils AI-driven eSource for clinical trial data.
news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio on March 20, 2025, Paradigm Health disclosed the addition of Karen E. Knudsen, MBA, PhD, to its Board of Directors as part of their efforts to revitalize the clinical research ecosystem. This announcement coincided with the imminent introduction of their innovative eSource application for clinical trial data management.

news.headline: Denison Mines Corp appoints former OPG CEO Ken Hartwick to its Board of Directors.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Denison Mines Corp. appointed Ken Hartwick to its Board of Directors. Hartwick, former CEO of Ontario Power Generation, joins the Board at a time when Denison's stock is traded on the TSX under DML and NYSE American under DNN. This move signifies a strategic step to enhance the Company's leadership with Hartwick's industry expertise.

news.headline: Study by Caris Life Sciences shows 20% eligible for tissue-agnostic therapies among 295,000+ patients. Expansion of approvals possible.
news.summary: A recent study involving over 295,000 patients found varying levels of effectiveness in treating different types of cancers with tissue-agnostic drugs. The research highlighted the possibility of extending approvals to similar drugs in the same category. This study was conducted in Irving, Texas, and the findings were released on March 20, 2025, by Caris Life Sciences®, a prominent organization in the field.

news.headline: VIAVI to showcase three new test solutions for AI infrastructure at booth 2319, OFC 2025.
news.summary: VIAVI Solutions Inc. will present three innovative products at booth 2319, aiding the shift towards advanced AI infrastructure. The unveiling will take place in CHANDLER, Arizona on March 20, 2025. VIAVI is listed on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol VIAV. The company's new test solutions for AI infrastructure will be highlighted at the event.

news.headline: Commercial Roofing Membrane Market to reach $238.2 million by 2035, growing at 3.4% CAGR. North America leads with 36.7% share.
news.summary: The Commercial Roofing Membrane Market was valued at USD 165.2 million in 2024 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3.4% to reach USD 238.2 million by 2035. The market is witnessing a transformation with the introduction of smart technology and sustainable materials, impacting energy efficiency and environmental concerns. The industry has evolved from traditional waterproofing to integrated solar and real-time monitoring systems. Europe leads in adopting smart membranes in corporate headquarters and retail complexes, while Asia-Pacific drives demand with rapid commercial infrastructure development. North America is estimated to hold a 36.7% market share by 2035, with an absolute $ opportunity of USD 25.4 million. Key drivers of market growth include building codes, sustainable construction practices, urbanization, energy efficiency requirements, extreme weather resilience, aging infrastructure renovation needs, and technological integration.

news.headline: K92 Mining appoints Robert Smillie as VP-Exploration, enhancing leadership team in exploration sector.
Alvotech acquires Xbrane Biopharma's R&D operations and biosimilar candidate XB003 for $27 million. Deal enhances development capabilities.
news.summary: Alvotech (NASDAQ: ALVO) recently disclosed their purchase of Xbrane Biopharma AB's R&D operations alongside a biosimilar candidate, XB003, modeled after Cimzia (certolizumab pegol), for approximately SEK275 million (around $27 million). The agreement involves a SEK102.2 million cash payment during closing, in addition to taking on SEK172.8 million in debt and accounts payable. Creditors have consented to receiving Alvotech equity shares for SEK152.8 million of the debt. This move enables Alvotech to bolster its development capabilities and signals its entry into Sweden's life sciences realm. Furthermore, the deal encompasses acquiring Xbrane's research facilities located at Campus Solna near the Karolinska Institute in Stockholm.

news.headline: First Hi-MO X10 Power Station in Shanghai surpassed expected power generation by 8% in Minhang District.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in Shanghai, the rooftop PV power station at Semir Apparel Co., Ltd. in Minhang District was officially linked to the grid. This marks the inauguration of the pioneering Hi-MO X10 distributed power station initiative in Shanghai. The station is a pivotal step towards sustainable energy with a significant capacity.

news.headline: Rock Tech Lithium partners with GEA for equipment in Guben Lithium Converter. Test works completed in Germany.
news.summary: Rock Tech Lithium has recently collaborated with GEA Group AG, a renowned global leader in process technology, to provide crystallization and zero-liquid discharge systems for its Lithium Converter located in Guben, Germany. The successful completion of test works has set the stage for the acquisition of crystallizers. This partnership marks a significant step forward for Rock Tech Lithium and its operations in the lithium industry.

news.headline: Yiren Digital (NYSE: YRD) announces Q4 2024 financial results in Beijing, offering financial and lifestyle services in China.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Yiren Digital Ltd. (NYSE: YRD), a leading China-based AI platform offering diverse financial and lifestyle services, revealed its unaudited financial performance for the quarter finishing in December.

news.headline: Gold Royalty Corp. reports record revenue and positive cash flows in Q4 2024, expecting significant growth in 2025 and next five years.
news.summary: In Vancouver, BC on March 20, 2025, Gold Royalty Corp. (NYSE American: GROY) shared its operating and financial results for the periods ending December 31, 2024. The company reported its performance for both the three-month and twelve-month durations. The figures discussed are in U.S. dollars.

news.headline: Allianz and Jio Financial reportedly strike deal for Indian joint venture.
Epson's RapidReceipt Scanners aid small businesses in tax season prep with AI, turning docs into digital assets for workflows.
news.summary: Epson's RapidReceipt scanners are helping small offices succeed by transforming documents into intelligent digital assets for AI processes. Located in Los Alamitos, California, this innovation is occurring as of March 20, 2025, The growing importance of AI in daily operations necessitates companies to make key investments in technology.

news.headline: H World Group Limited releases 2024 financial results. Key player in global hotel industry.
news.summary: Singapore and Shanghai, March 20, 2025 - H World Group Limited, a prominent company in the global hotel industry, revealed its unaudited financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on December 31, 2024. Traded under the tickers NASDAQ: HTHT and HKEX: 1179, the company shared important data concerning its financial performance.

news.headline: Armada Hoffler has filled nearly all the vacant WeWork space at The Interlock in Atlanta with three new office leases.
news.summary: The majority of available space at WeWork has been leased out following recent transactions at a prominent mixed-use development. Nearly all previously vacant WeWork spaces have now been filled, indicating a successful outcome for the development. This latest round of leasing activities has resulted in a high occupancy rate, with only a small fraction of the WeWork spaces remaining unoccupied. The success of these transactions highlights the strong demand for workspace in the development, with a significant number of new tenants now occupying the previously vacant WeWork areas.

news.headline: Shoe Carnival reported Q4 and Fiscal 2024 results: GAAP EPS of $2.68, Adjusted EPS of $2.72, annual sales growth of 2.3%.
news.summary: Shoe Carnival, Inc., a prominent footwear and accessories retailer, announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending on February 1, 2025. The company, listed on Nasdaq as SCVL, reported that in Fiscal 2024, they achieved an EPS of $2.68 on a GAAP basis and $2.72 on an Adjusted basis, meeting the high end of annual expectations. Additionally, they experienced a 2.3 percent growth in annual sales. Looking ahead to Fiscal 2025, the company provided guidance for the fiscal year ending on January 31, 2026.

news.headline: Ampion partners with Erie County, NY to offer up to 10% electricity savings via community solar to low-income residents.
news.summary: Erie County in New York has announced Ampion Renewable Energy as the administrator for the Erie County Low Income Program for Sustainable Energy (ECLIPSE). This initiative aims to offer electricity savings to residents in need. Ampion, in partnership with Erie County, will assist eligible residents in accessing up to 10% savings on electricity through community solar. Interested individuals can sign up for the program through Ampion and benefit from discounted solar credits applied to their bills.

news.headline: Trump believes the Fed should reduce rates to offset the impact of tariffs.
QXO to acquire Beacon Roofing Supply for $11 billion, with $124.35 per share in cash, creating a leading roofing distributor.
news.summary: In Greenwich, Conn. and Herndon, Va., QXO, Inc. (NYSE: QXO) and Beacon Roofing Supply, Inc. (Nasdaq: BECN) have confirmed a merger deal. QXO is set to purchase Beacon at a price of $124.35 per share in cash. Known for its distribution of roofing, waterproofing, and exterior products, Beacon boasts close to 600 branches spanning the United States and Canada. The acquisition has received unanimous approval from both companies' boards of directors. This significant transaction marks a notable valuation for Beacon.

news.headline: Vena's Excelerate Finance 2025 conference in Las Vegas from May 12-15 focuses on FP&A growth.
news.summary: Vena, a leading Complete Planning platform integrated with Microsoft 365, has revealed the return of Excelerate Finance for this year. The event, Excelerate 2025, will be held at The Wynn Hotel in Las Vegas from May 12-15. It aims to gather finance professionals, business leaders, analysts, and experts worldwide to brainstorm the future of FP&A and the office of finance. This conference is set to be bigger and better than before, offering valuable insights and networking opportunities for attendees.

news.headline: dunnhumby partners with Osmos to improve retail media, enhancing data science for retailers.
news.summary: In Cincinnati, dunnhumby, a prominent figure in customer data science globally, has recently unveiled a new addition to its retail media ecosystem. The company has collaborated with Osmos, an expert in advanced ad-serving technology, on the dunnhumby Retail Media Platform. This partnership is particularly significant as retailers are currently aiming to optimize the effectiveness of their retail media networks. Through the incorporation of Osmos' technology into its current retail media services, dunnhumby strengthens its position in the market.
